reviews.author: Sabri Kürüm
reviews.text: Amazing views. Not an easy climb but at the sane time not too hard. Just have light clothes and a bottle of water with you. You can enjoy the mountains, rice fields at various heights. Best to go in the morning to beat the crowds and the heat

reviews.author: Sarah Hasselman
reviews.text: We came at the start of January 2026. As others have stated, beware the parking scams surrounding the area. The price to enter is 100,000VND. Even if you don't plan to climb, it's worth it to enter and walk around. There are nice ponds with fish and kingfishers, boardwalks through paddies, cafés, toilets, shops and caves inside the ticket area. You can actually visit the cave. It's pretty big and there's seating inside which I imagine is nice in the summer. But, most people visit for the climb up the 500 steps of stairs for the amazing views of the area. I hike weekly so the climb is not a challenge at all, but I will say it's steeper than I expected and I was glad to have hiking shoes for grip, especially at the top. The area around the Dragon statue at the summit is treacherous. Please wear good shoes and bring water. There is a shop selling water and other drinks near the top. If you're fast you can climb up easily in 15 minutes, but if you're stopping to rest or take photos it's more like 20-30 minutes. It will be longer if you come late because you will need to dodge crowds. It was empty at 8:20AM when we arrived but by the time we left at 10AM the buses of tourists had arrived. So I definitely recommend arriving before 9AM.

reviews.author: Utkersh Bora ubmuse
reviews.text: If you’re even slightly a mountain person, let’s clear this up right now: the Hang Múa climb is not that hard. It’s a steady staircase, nothing dramatic, nothing soul-crushing. Your legs will survive. Your ego will remain intact. The climb is actually quite enjoyable — scenic, well-paced, and just enough effort to make you feel like you earned what’s coming next. And what’s coming next is… WOW. The views are classic Ninh Binh magic: winding rivers, green fields, limestone karsts doing their cinematic thing like they were born for postcards. Now the real challenge begins at the top. Not the height — the crowd. It’s busy. Like strategic elbow placement busy. Everyone wants the same photo, the same dragon pose, the same “I am alone with nature” illusion… while 200 people stand politely out of frame waiting their turn. The energy up there is less meditation, more organized chaos. Still, the view is so good you forgive everything. You wait, you dodge, you smile, you click photos, and you silently agree that yes — it was worth it.

reviews.author: Ina
reviews.text: Amazing Place, must see when you are in Ninh binh. We went there with a small group for a whole day experience. Just a few steps, but better wear sneakers or something similar.

reviews.author: VânLan Nguyen
reviews.text: Standing atop the jagged, dragon-shaped ridge of Ngoạ Long Mountain, the world below unfolds in a breathtaking tapestry of mist and stone. The panoramic view captures the essence of Trang An's ethereal beauty, where limestone karsts rise dramatically from the calm, mirror-like waters of the Ngô Đồng River. From this vantage point, the golden rice paddies form a patchwork quilt against the lush green valleys, and tiny boats drift silently through the winding waterways like fallen leaves. The climb is steep, but the reward is a moment of pure serenity, suspended between the ancient earth and the endless sky, truly earning its name as the realm of the "Lying Dragon" (Ngoạ Long)! Tràng An 12/2025

reviews.author: Phạm Ngọc
reviews.text: Very worth the climb! But i really recommend wearing your hiking shoes or anything similar because the stone steps are quite steep and can get slippery, especially when there is just a slight rain. Aside from the climb, you can walk around and watch the scenery. I wandered around and met this kitten sleeping!!!!!!!

reviews.author: Melissa Wilson
reviews.text: The view is ok. Probably the highest view around here. The pollution in the air makes it hard to see very far. Got there at 6.30am and reached the top in 20 minutes. Not a difficult walk. Probably about 20 people up there when we arrived. Would hate to see it later in the day with the masses. Nice lotus gardens at the bottom are quite pretty with the birds.

reviews.author: Simon Nolan
reviews.text: The gardens and area is real chill and good for photos. The climb up is like 500 steps no one no less😝 views were great. Well worth a visit.

reviews.author: Annie C.
reviews.text: The 600+ steps to the top are absolutely worth it! The views from the summit are breathtaking — and so is the climb itself. 😅 But once you reach the top, the panoramic view of Ninh Binh’s limestone peaks and rice fields is unforgettable. Bring water, wear good shoes, and take your time — it’s a must-do! 🫶

reviews.author: Hằng
reviews.text: When I came back to Mua Caves, I didn’t climb Dragon Mountain because it was too exhausting. I just hung around the lotus lake — it was lotus season, so the flowers were blooming beautifully. GG didn’t approve my photos🌶️

reviews.author: Vivekananda Nareddula
reviews.text: Hang Mua is absolutely worth a visit! The climb is a bit challenging with around 500 stone steps, but once you reach the top, the panoramic view of Ninh Binh’s limestone mountains and rice fields is breathtaking. It’s one of the best viewpoints in Vietnam! Go early in the morning or near sunset for cooler weather and fewer crowds. Wear comfortable shoes and carry water — the climb is steep but the reward at the top is totally worth it.

reviews.author: G A
reviews.text: Loved this place! A definite highlight of our trip to Ninh Bình. There are many photo spots and scenic areas to explore. If you have the endurance, climbing up the mountain is totally worth it—the view at the top is stunning and absolutely Instagram-worthy. Of the three spots we visited today, this is the one I’d recommend the most. It has a good mix of nature, adventure, and relaxation. Come and enjoy!

reviews.author: Monica Tinna
reviews.text: 6 Mar 2024 The weather was okay and in Lotus Lake, the lotuses were not blooming. So we walked ahead and went to the cave. The cave was short, and later we went up to the dragon lying, and we were amazed at the view. We were at the top on only one side, and on the other side, we didn't go there. I am a travel agent and traveller you can contact me on Instagram ID Personal ID: mystry_scrt Business ID: mercijourney

reviews.author: Lily H.
reviews.text: There is now a 100,000 dong entrance fee per person, but it’s worth it! The views are beautiful, and there are many photo ops around the cafes at the bottom. The lotus fields are gorgeous, too. Highly recommended!

reviews.author: NiYaS SrK
reviews.text: Stunning view from above as well as from down the hill, 400 plus steps worth climbing. Garden view calm and neat atmosphere with great restaurant and photography points. Lotus field, great for taking picture. Must visit

reviews.author: Nancy Trekker
reviews.text: Beautiful viewpoint about 10 minutes from Tam Coc, with a climb of around 60 meters of stairs (about six floors). I arrived right when the gates opened at 5:30 a.m., which was a great time to visit before it got busy. It’s clearly a very popular attraction, and tour buses began arriving as I was leaving. The views are lovely, but Northern Vietnam has many incredible viewpoints, so visiting early is the best way to enjoy this one.

reviews.author: Svan
reviews.text: Extraordinary views. 100kr fair value for the facilities at the bottom and the way up to the mountain. Not as bad of a climbs as people make it out to be. I did this the day after recovering from a pretty though food poisoning. Took about 20 minutes to get up. Bit more time if you go midday and in the tourist buss rush from Hanoi. Went for sunset, not as hot, let crowded and had an amazing sunset of course. Lots of photo opportunities. I advise being a bit careful on the very last bit up to the dragon though. It’s razor sharp rocks with huge drops on each side. A Guy almost fell down on our time up there. Parking with a scooter can be done all the way by the gate. People will try to stop you faaarfar down the road. Drive past them if you don’t want to walk 10 minutes extra. Bring lots of water

reviews.author: M. Jenson
reviews.text: Amazing view of the area. However, it can be very difficult to get to the dragon since there is no proper way to get up there and people in general do not care about other people’s safety. There should be a guard to help making lines so it would be easier to get back and forth. Not recommended for elderly to climb the dragon. They also have a really beautiful lotus garden.

reviews.author: Michiel de Groot
reviews.text: Absolutely stunning and well worth it. Don't get scammed by the bike people. You can park your bike for 5k dong right outside the ticket office. They try to stop you on the way there, but you can just keep going until you see the ticket office and then park on the right next to it.

reviews.author: Mateo Guzmán
reviews.text: A very cool touristic attraction in Vietnam. To go there you can rent a scooter from your hotel in Ninh Bin or another town and you can park it anywhere there (I’m not sure if there is free parking but I paid 15k as there was a women offering it at the entrance) To go to the view point you need to go up like 500 stairs steps, there are some places in between to rest and take some photos. Once you get up there the view is totally worth it. Once you descend again, there are many shops where you can eat and refresh yourself. There are also free toilets which are very clean too.

reviews.author: Elias Bisharat
reviews.text: Very nice place with 500 steps for an amazing view VERY RECOMMENDED

reviews.author: Rizwan Sohail
reviews.text: dragon on top of the hill, not a difficult climb for those doing cardio, coming down can be a bit painful if you are older. good view from the top. Winds were high and it was raining when we went but it was alright, it wasnt slippery. Take the left route to reach the top.

reviews.author: Axzioma Adhitia
reviews.text: I was charged 10K for parking, and I think it's a scam. This place is actually just an ordinary hill, in my opinion, but with the stairs and new buildings deliberately built as a tourist attraction, I don't see anything authentic about it. It's just overrated.

reviews.author: Food lover 8888
reviews.text: Amazing place to visit! The lotus field… flying dragon mountain. Everything was a great experience!

reviews.author: Mathias Skjæran Landrø
reviews.text: Very amazing viewpoint! So amazing to see the limestone cliffs go straight up from the rice fields! Really loved this place! It is a bit touristy tho, but worth it! On you way there if you bicycle or go by motorbike, there is a lot of people who try to force you to their parking lot. But you should go all the way to the end

reviews.author: Hashim Fakhreddin
reviews.text: Stunning views at the the peak. Be prepared to sweat a lot as its hot and humid. Bring hiking clothes and shoes. About 500 steps to the top and they are uneven steps some bigger than others. There are a few resting points and a shop at about the half way point that sells cool drinks. It can get a little busy at the very top, even early in the morning but not too bad. People were generally nice and helpful. If you're lucky you'll see some goats! Also the cold coconut drinks at the bottom are a nice refreshing end to your hike.

reviews.author: Cara Doherty
reviews.text: A lovely (if challenging) set of steps but the staff here are rude. There are lots of people outside trying to get you to park your bike in their parking space. I asked the lady at the ticket desk how much I should expect to pay for parking, so I could ensure I paid a fair price and she refused to offer a ball park figure. She was grumpy, cold and unhelpful. FYI: In the end we paid 10,000. The restaurant staff were similarly cold and it was a bit of a shambles. The lady at the till had no customer service skills. We were next in the queue and after she served the previous customer she ignored us, counting money and chatting to other staff instead. We waited at the counter for around 10 minutes to be served. During this time she did not acknowledge us to let us know she was aware we were waiting, despite being right there. This was the middle of the day so it’s not like she was just opening up the till.

reviews.author: Md. Maruf Hossain
reviews.text: Ninh Bình, often referred to as the "Halong Bay on Land," is a stunning destination in northern Vietnam. Known for its dramatic landscapes of limestone karsts, lush rice paddies, and serene waterways, it offers a breathtaking escape into nature. The area is rich in cultural heritage, featuring ancient temples, traditional villages, and historical sites such as the ancient capital of Hoa Lu. A boat ride through the tranquil rivers of Tam Coc or Trang An provides an unforgettable experience, showcasing the region's natural beauty and peaceful ambience.

reviews.author: B Roy Chaudhary
reviews.text: Hang Mua is the name of the whole complex with ticket office (100k in Nov25 pp, 5k for bicycle - I left bike unchained - was fine), restaurants, eco lodges, landscaped ponds with trees and bonsai, a long wooden walkway along spread out lotus ponds, caves and the the main steps (approx 500) leading up the mountain with a fork going to two peaks - the one with dragon is higher).

reviews.author: Sidney Stanbury
reviews.text: Very enjoyable. The climb is much shorter than described in some comments. There are two peaks, each a short 5 minute climb from a middle platform which one reaches in roughly 5-15 minutes. The (small) effort is definitely worth it, you well spend much more time at the top than on your way to it!
